steps.title: Build the transformed function
steps.content:
Given \(f(x) = 24x^3\), replace \(x\) with \(-x\):
$$f(-x) = 24(-x)^3 = -24x^3$$
Adding the constant \(c\) gives:
$$y = -24x^3 + c$$

steps.title: Find the y-intercept (set x = 0)
steps.content:
$$y = -24(0)^3 + c = c$$
Since \(c\) is a positive integer, \(t = c > 0\).

steps.title: Evaluate each choice
steps.content:
• Choice A — '\(r < 0\) and \(t > 0\)': We showed \(r > 0\), not negative. ✗
  • Choice B — '\(r > 0\) and \(t > 0\)': Both \(r = \sqrt[3]{c/24} > 0\) and \(t = c > 0\). ✓
  • Choice C — '\(r > 0\) and \(t < 0\)': \(t = c > 0\), so \(t\) cannot be negative. ✗
  • Choice D — '\(r < 0\) and \(t < 0\)': Both conclusions are wrong; \(r > 0\) and \(t > 0\). ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
